children.title: Short title and commencement.
children.text:
Short title and commencement.
(1) This Act may be called the Public Records Act, 1993.
(2) It shall come into force on such date as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Definitions.
Power of the Central Government to coordinate, regulate and supervise operations connected with administration, management etc., of public records.
children.text:
Power of the Central Government to coordinate, regulate and supervise operations connected with administration, management etc., of public records.
(1) The Central Government shall have the power to coordinate, regulate and supervise the operations connected with the administration, management, conservation, preservation, disposal, custody and use of public records.
(2) The Director General shall exercise the powers of the Central Government under sub-section (1) in relation to—
(a) the records of the Central Government and of such other records creating agencies which are specified in sub-clause (ii) of clause (f) of section 2;
(b) advising records creating agencies in the framing of records retention and disposal schedules.
(3) The head of the Archives shall exercise such powers of the Central Government under sub-section (1) in relation to the records of the Union territory Administration as may be directed by the Central Government.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Prohibition against taking of public records out of India.
children.text:
Prohibition against taking of public records out of India.
No person shall take or cause to be taken out of India any public records without the prior approval of the Central Government:
Provided that no such approval shall be required if any public records are taken out of India for any official purpose.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Records officer.
children.text:
Records officer.
(1) Every records creating agency shall nominate one of its officers as the records officer to function in that agency for the purposes of this Act.
(2) Every records officer so nominated shall be responsible for the proper arrangement, maintenance, and preservation of public records under his charge and shall exercise such powers, perform such duties, and discharge such functions as may be prescribed.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Responsibilities of records officer.
Unauthorised removal or destruction of public records.
children.text:
Unauthorised removal or destruction of public records.
Every records officer shall take such measures for the protection and security of public records in his custody as may be prescribed and, in the event of any unauthorised removal, destruction or defacement of, or damage to any such records, shall take immediate action for their recovery or replacement, as the case may be, and report the matter to his superior officer.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Destruction or disposal of public records.
children.text:
Destruction or disposal of public records.
No public records shall be destroyed or otherwise disposed of except in such manner and subject to such conditions as may be prescribed.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Penalty.
children.text:
Penalty.
Whoever contravenes any provision of this Act or any rule made thereunder shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to five years or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees or with both.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Public records bearing security classification.
children.text:
Public records bearing security classification.
(1) Every records creating agency shall specify the period for which the security classification of any public records shall remain in force.
(2) No records creating agency shall withhold any public records from being transferred to the National Archives of India or the Archives of the Union territory Administration, as the case may be, on the ground of security classification:
Provided that such records shall be made available only to bona fide research scholars with the approval of the head of the department concerned.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Access to public records.
children.text:
Access to public records.
(1) Subject to any law for the time being in force, all public records in the National Archives of India or the Archives of any Union territory Administration which are more than thirty years old may be, with the approval of the Director General or the head of the Archives, as the case may be, made available to any bona fide research scholar for study and reference.
(2) The Central Government may by general or special order direct that any public records, which are less than thirty years old, be made available to any bona fide research scholar if such records are not of a classified nature.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Acceptance of records of private nature.
children.text:
Acceptance of records of private nature.
The Director General or the head of the Archives, as the case may be, may accept any records of private nature, whether by way of gift, bequest, transfer or otherwise, which in his opinion are of historical interest or value.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Archival Advisory Board.
children.text:
Archival Advisory Board.
(1) The Central Government shall constitute a Board, to be called the Archival Advisory Board, to advise the Central Government on matters relating to the administration and management of public records in general and to perform such functions as may be prescribed.
(2) The Board shall consist of—
(a) a Chairperson to be nominated by the Central Government;
(b) such number of other members, not exceeding fifteen, as may be prescribed;
(c) the Director General, who shall be the Member-Secretary.
(3) The terms and conditions of appointment and the manner of filling vacancies among the members of the Board shall be such as may be prescribed.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Offences by companies.
children.text:
Offences by companies.
(1) Where an offence under this Act has been committed by a company, every person who at the time the offence was committed was in charge of, and was responsible to, the company for the conduct of the business of the company, as well as the company, shall be deemed to be guilty of the offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly:
Provided that nothing contained in this sub-section shall render any such person liable to any punishment provided in this Act if he proves that the offence was committed without his knowledge or that he had exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of such offence.
(2) Notwithstanding anything contained in sub-section (1), where an offence under this Act has been committed by a company and it is proved that the offence has been committed with the consent or connivance of, or is attributable to any neglect on the part of, any director, manager, secretary or other officer of the company, such director, manager, secretary or other officer shall also be deemed to be guilty of that offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly.
Explanation.— For the purposes of this section,—
(a) "company" means any body corporate and includes a firm or other association of individuals; and
(b) "director", in relation to a firm, means a partner in the firm.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Power to make rules.
Power to remove difficulties.
children.text:
Power to remove difficulties.
(1) If any difficulty arises in giving effect to the provisions of this Act, the Central Government may, by order, published in the Official Gazette, make such provisions not inconsistent with the provisions of this Act as may appear to be necessary for removing the difficulty:
Provided that no order shall be made under this section after the expiry of two years from the date of commencement of this Act.
(2) Every order made under this section shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before each House of Parliament.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
